package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "net/http"
    "os"
)

// Negative scenario for world_readable_file_permissions: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `os.OpenFile(path, flag, 0666)` or `os.WriteFile(path, data, 0777)` with world-readable/writable permissions
type CaseWorldReadableFilePermissionsDTO struct {
    ID       string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status   string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount   int    `json:"amount"`
    Payload  []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseWorldReadableFilePermissionsModel struct {
    ID        string       `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID  string       `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status    string       `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseWorldReadableFilePermissionsRepository struct {
    db    *gorm.DB
    sql   *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseWorldReadableFilePermissionsModel
}

type CaseWorldReadableFilePermissionsService struct {
    repo   *CaseWorldReadableFilePermissionsRepository
    client *http.Client
}

type CaseWorldReadableFilePermissionsAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeWorldReadableFilePermissions(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseWorldReadableFilePermissionsDTO) error {
    // Restrictive file permissions — only owner can read/write.
    _ = os.WriteFile("/tmp/config.json", []byte(input), 0600)
    f, err := os.OpenFile("/tmp/secrets.txt", os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0644)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    defer f.Close()
    _, _ = f.WriteString(input)
    return nil
}
